title: On A Dial.
content:
1

    To-morrow and to-morrow
        Is but to-day:
    The world wags but to borrow
        Time that grows gray: - 
    Grammercy! time's but sorrow
        And - well away!


    2

    Since time hales but to sadness
        And to decay,
    Men needs wax fools for madness,
        Laugh, curse, and pray;
    Death grapples with their badness - 
        The Devil's to pay.
meaning:
"On A Dial." is a quintessential example of Madison Julius Cawein's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
